description: AI-DLC worked example: agent-job-matcher, derived from its OpenSpec markdown (openspec/changes/<name>/ = Intent, "## Bolt N" = Bolt, "- [x]" = Unit of Work). IMPORTANT: the source contains NO estimation data of any kind — no story points, hours or complexity ratings. FP type/complexity here were inferred by keyword heuristic as a FIRST PASS FOR HUMAN REVIEW, not measured. Treat the totals as illustrative of the tool, not as an estimate of this project.

projectSummary.name: agent-job-matcher
projectSummary.tagline: Resume-to-posting matching, built end-to-end with AI-DLC
projectSummary.description: A job-matching agent (resume parsing, posting fetch, LLM scoring, cover-letter generation) delivered following the full AI-DLC method: 7 Intents, 31 Bolts, 190 Units of Work, with evals pinned before or alongside the code they gate. Used here as a structural reference for what an AI-native delivery breakdown looks like in practice.

valuePropositions: Worked example of an AI-DLC delivery breakdown, end to end
Bolts sized as deployable increments rather than time-boxed sprints
Evals specified before the code they gate, as first-class units of work
Structure derived from real OpenSpec artifacts, not invented for a demo

EI.name: External Input
EI.description: An elementary process that processes data or control information from outside the application boundary.

EO.name: External Output
EO.description: An elementary process that generates data or control information sent outside the application boundary.

EQ.name: External Inquiry
EQ.description: An elementary process that retrieves data from ILF/EIF without calculations or derived data.

ILF.name: Internal Logical File
ILF.description: A user-identifiable group of logically related data maintained within the application boundary.

EIF.name: External Interface File
EIF.description: A user-identifiable group of logically related data referenced but not maintained by the application.

gscDefinitions.name: Data Communications
gscDefinitions.description: Degree to which the application communicates with external systems

gscDefinitions.name: Distributed Data Processing
gscDefinitions.description: Degree to which data is transferred between components

gscDefinitions.name: Performance
gscDefinitions.description: User-stated response time or throughput objectives

gscDefinitions.name: Heavily Used Configuration
gscDefinitions.description: Degree to which hardware restrictions influence the application

gscDefinitions.name: Transaction Rate
gscDefinitions.description: Rate at which transactions are processed

gscDefinitions.name: Online Update
gscDefinitions.description: Degree of online update for ILFs

gscDefinitions.name: Reusability
gscDefinitions.description: Degree to which application is designed for reuse

gscDefinitions.name: Operational Ease
gscDefinitions.description: Ease of operation and minimal intervention

aiAssisted.label: AI-Assisted Delivery
aiAssisted.description: Middle of the delivery continuum — human-led build with an AI agent assisting. The human still owns the work.

aiNative.label: AI-Native Delivery (agent-led)
aiNative.description: Far end of the continuum — the agent produces the bulk and the human steers, reviews and approves. ILLUSTRATIVE AND UNCALIBRATED: 0.03 against a nominal 8 h/FP reconstructs a ~15 min/FP working figure. Replace with a measured value once pre-estimates and actuals have been captured.

prototyping.label: Rapid Prototyping
prototyping.description: POC/demo depth rather than production hardening.

sdlcPhases.name: Requirements & Analysis
sdlcPhases.description: Business analysis, user stories, acceptance criteria

sdlcPhases.name: Design & Architecture
sdlcPhases.description: UI/UX design, system design, API specifications

beta.label: Approved
beta.description: Approved at the Inception Gate, construction not complete.

glossary.definition: The Inception ceremony where the plan is drafted; the owner reviews and requests changes.
The approval point where open questions must be settled before construction starts.
The answer key for grading an eval — criteria and procedure, written before the code exists.
HARD evals block progress; SOFT evals are advisory.
